title: The cat I looked after in Lyon
summary: A short story about caring for a friend's pet while they were away, and how it changed your week.
topic: The cat I looked after in Lyon
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a pet you cared for while a friend was away. Describe the routine, one small thing that went wrong, and how you felt at the end of the week. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: Last November, my friend Camille went to…
Every morning before work, I walked across the river to…
On the third evening, I could not find him anywhere…
sampleText: Last November, my friend Camille went to a wedding in Marseille for ten days and asked me to look after her cat, Tonton. He was a fat orange tabby who slept on the windowsill and only liked one kind of food. Every morning before work, I walked across the Saône to her tiny apartment on rue Mercière, fed him, cleaned the litter box, and sat with him for fifteen minutes while he purred against my leg. By the fourth morning, I could tell from the way he scratched the door that he was waiting for me. On the third evening, however, I could not find him anywhere. I called his name, opened cupboards, looked under the bed, and started to panic. While I was checking the balcony for the second time, I heard a small thump from the wardrobe. He had pushed the door open and fallen asleep on a pile of Camille's winter scarves. When Camille came back, she hugged me too long at the door. 'He doesn't usually like new people,' she said. 'You must have done something right.' I had not done anything special. I had just shown up at the same hour, every day, with the same patient voice.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: asked me to look after her cat
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhờ tôi trông con mèo của cô ấy
một con mèo vằn vàng béo

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I walked across the Saône to her tiny apartment
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đi qua sông Saône đến căn hộ nhỏ của cô ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: while he purred against my leg
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trong khi nó kêu gừ gừ vào chân tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I could not find him anywhere
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi không thể tìm thấy nó ở đâu cả

sampleAnnotations.phrase: While I was checking the balcony
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Trong khi tôi đang kiểm tra ban công

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He had pushed the door open and fallen asleep
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Nó đã đẩy cửa mở ra và ngủ thiếp đi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: You must have done something right
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cậu chắc đã làm gì đó đúng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Must have + past participle' for past deduction
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'You must have done something right' dùng modal hoàn thành để suy đoán về một sự việc đã xảy ra trong quá khứ — người nói chắc chắn, dựa trên bằng chứng hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'You must have done something right' uses a perfect modal to make a confident deduction about a completed past event, based on present evidence.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an earlier past event
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'He had pushed the door open and fallen asleep' dùng past perfect vì hành động này xảy ra trước thời điểm tôi tìm thấy nó (past simple 'heard').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'He had pushed the door open and fallen asleep' uses past perfect because the action happened before the moment I found him (past simple 'heard').

sampleTranslationVi: Tháng Mười Một năm ngoái, bạn tôi Camille đi dự đám cưới ở Marseille trong mười ngày và nhờ tôi trông con mèo của cô ấy, Tonton. Nó là một con mèo vằn vàng béo, ngủ trên bậu cửa sổ và chỉ thích một loại thức ăn duy nhất. Mỗi sáng trước khi đi làm, tôi đi qua sông Saône đến căn hộ nhỏ của cô ấy ở phố Mercière, cho nó ăn, dọn khay vệ sinh, và ngồi với nó mười lăm phút trong khi nó kêu gừ gừ vào chân tôi. Đến sáng thứ tư, tôi có thể nhận ra qua cách nó cào cửa rằng nó đang đợi tôi. Tối thứ ba, tuy nhiên, tôi không thể tìm thấy nó ở đâu cả. Tôi gọi tên nó, mở các tủ, nhìn dưới giường, và bắt đầu hoảng loạn. Trong khi tôi đang kiểm tra ban công lần thứ hai, tôi nghe một tiếng thịch nhỏ từ tủ quần áo. Nó đã đẩy cửa mở ra và ngủ thiếp đi trên một đống khăn mùa đông của Camille. Khi Camille trở về, cô ôm tôi quá lâu ở cửa. 'Bình thường nó không thích người lạ,' cô nói. 'Cậu chắc đã làm gì đó đúng.' Tôi không làm gì đặc biệt. Tôi chỉ xuất hiện đúng giờ, mỗi ngày, với cùng giọng nói kiên nhẫn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'look after'.
options: She asked me to look after her cat.
She asked me to look at her cat.
She asked me to look in her cat.
She asked me to look on her cat.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct routine sentence in past simple.
options: Every morning, I walked across the river, fed him, and cleaned the litter box.
Every morning, I walk across the river, feed him, and clean the litter box.
Every morning, I was walked across the river, fed him, and cleaned the litter box.
Every morning, I have walk across the river, fed him, and cleaned the litter box.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple chain ('walked, fed, cleaned') for a past routine over ten days.

options: the soft sound a happy cat makes in its throat
the angry sound a dog makes
the sound of a door opening
the cry of a baby
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Purr' = the low vibrating sound of a content cat.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While I was checking the balcony, I heard a small thump.
While I checked the balcony, I was heard a small thump.
While I check the balcony, I heard a small thump.
While I am checking the balcony, I heard a small thump.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous for background; past simple for the inserted sound.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect sentence.
options: He had pushed the door open and fallen asleep on the scarves.
He pushed the door open and fallen asleep on the scarves.
He has pushed the door open and fall asleep on the scarves.
He was push the door open and fell asleep on the scarves.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had pushed... and fallen' for an event before another past event.

options: to suddenly feel very scared and not know what to do
to walk very fast
to think very slowly
to fall asleep quickly

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with the modal 'must have'.
options: You must have done something right.
You must did something right.
You must do something right.
You must have do something right.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Must have + past participle' = strong past deduction.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'slept ___ the windowsill'.
options: on
in
at
by
He doesn't usually like new people.
He doesn't likes usually new people.
He don't usually likes new people.
He usually doesn't likes new people.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'usually' goes between auxiliary 'doesn't' and main verb 'like' (base form after does/doesn't).

options: to arrive somewhere as expected
to display a photo
to leave a place
to shout loudly

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural closing line.
options: I had just shown up at the same hour, every day, with the same patient voice.
I just shown up at the same hour, every day, with the same patient voice.
I had just show up at the same hour, every day, with the same patient voice.
I have just shown up at the same hour, every day, with the same patient voice.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had shown up' for a repeated past behaviour reflecting back from the closing moment.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do animals often trust people who simply keep a routine, more than people who try hard to be liked?
